events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Scenario: field service, Automotive & Powertrain, Fluids & leaks. Observed field condition: milkshake oil cap (FD). Work rough-complete. Need identify component or condition and visible defect cues. "milkshake oil cap" means oil filler cap has milky, frothy emulsion indicating coolant mixing with engine oil, likely head gasket failure, cracked head/block, or condensation. In installed operating asset, rough-complete. Condition category installed-defective. Decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous? It indicates engine internal coolant leak, potential severe damage, not acceptable. Risk high/critical? Coolant in oil causes bearing damage, engine failure. Could be critical. Need output JSON with fields. Need include findings: milky emulsion under oil filler cap, coolant contamination of engine oil, possible head gasket/cylinder head/EGR cooler/ oil cooler leak. Actions: do not run engine, sample oil, pressure test cooling system, inspect head gasket/cylinder head, repair source, flush oil, replace oil/filter. Rationale. References: OEM specs. Need fill all fields. work_stage rough-complete. percent_complete maybe 80? component_conditions installed-defective. value 0. sound_source etc default empty string/0. order empty. workable empty. confidence 0.95. event_time 0.0 rate 0.0. Ensure valid JSON.

  "findings": [
    "Milky, frothy 'milkshake' residue on underside of oil filler cap",
    "Coolant contamination of engine lubricating oil",
    "Indication of internal coolant-to-oil leak or excessive condensation",
    "Potential engine damage risk if operated"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not run or release the asset until cause is confirmed",
    "Inspect oil dipstick and oil condition for further coolant contamination",
    "Pressure-test the cooling system per OEM procedure",
    "Inspect cylinder head, head gasket, EGR cooler, and oil cooler for leaks",
    "Repair identified leak source and flush contaminated oil",
    "Replace engine oil and filter, then verify oil and coolant integrity"
  ],
  "rationale": "A milkshake-like residue on the oil filler cap indicates coolant has mixed with engine oil, an internal leak condition that can rapidly destroy bearings and cause catastrophic engine failure. Because the asset is installed and operating, this is a critical defect requiring immediate shutdown and diagnosis before close-out.",
  "references": ["OEM specs"]
}
